octopus-salad-with-chickpeas-and-pepper-.name: Octopus Salad With Chickpeas And Pepper
octopus-salad-with-chickpeas-and-pepper-.original_name: Salada de polvo com grão e pimento
instructions: Place the octopus in a pot, add the peeled onion and the blonde leaf and cover with water. Take it to the fire, let it boil and boil for 40 minutes, or until the octopus becomes soft and tender.

Brush the octopus, let it cool and cut it into pieces; cut the tomato into small pieces; peel the cucumber and cut it into cubes; clean the peppers and cut them into cubes as well. Put the vegetables in a salad bowl.

Add the leek, cut into thick slices, and the grain, previously washed and drained. Add the octopus, season with salt and pepper, water with olive oil and sprinkle with chopped parsley. Mix gently and serve.

old-hermit-cake.name: Old Hermit Cake
instructions: Preheat oven to 135°C (135 degrees C).
Cream butter and sugar together well. Beat in eggs one at a time. Mix in lemon juice and vanilla. Add remaining ingredients. Stir well.
Turn into greased and floured 10 inch angel food tube pan. Bake in oven for about 2 1/2 to 3 hours until an inserted wooden pick comes out clean.

olive-bread-recipe-comedera.name: Olive Bread, Recipe

ingredients.item: 100 g of bone free black olives (you can also use green)

instructions: In a bowl, we will add the flour, salt and yeast, we revere to integrate everything. We open up a space in the middle of dry ingredients to add water little by little. With the help of our hands, we're going to amass up and get a consistent mass.
Filling your counter and place the dough to add the other ingredients. Stretch and add olives and olive oil. Kill her for five minutes till she's all been incorporated.
We make our dough a ball, we put it in a bowl and cover it with a moist cloth to let it stand for 30 minutes.
After that time, we take our dough to give shape to the bread. Since regularly it looks rustic with its bark, we recommend that you make it oval. with a knife do some openings to his surface.
Put them in a plate with waxed paper and cover them with a wet cloth and we'll let them ferment for 1 hour.
That's about 5 minutes to complete the fermentation process. We'll have to get our oven up to an oc.o.
Let's put our bread to bake for 20 minutes, till you see the golden color and that clear smell of olives.
We're through and we'll enjoy ourselves!

omelet-muffins-argiro.name: Omelet Muffins

ingredients.item: 10 cherry tomatoes cut into small frames
A little olive oil for the forms
10 cherry tomatoes cut into small frames
A little olive oil for the forms

instructions: Beat the eggs into a bowl with the wire and add salt and pepper at will.
Finely chop all vegetables, ham and graviera into small frames.
Add them to the mixture with the eggs and mix.
Add a brush to a pan with six cases for muffins and balance the mixture.
Bake the orlet muffins in a well preheated oven at 170°C, in the air, in the medium grill for 20 minutes, until stabilized.
Let them cool down and let them out.
Serve with salad.
If the children take them to school, we escort them with whole-grain crackers and tomatoes. Complete with blueberries (or any other fruit we have) to complete the school meal properly.

one-bowl-upside-down-cake.name: One-bowl Upside-down Cake

ingredients.item: -ounce bags frozen sweet cherries or blueberries
Plus 1 cup sugar; plus more for serving
Sour cream or whole plain yogurt

instructions: Arrange a rack in center of oven; preheat to 425°. Take this time to assemble all your ingredients and do your prep. Melt ½ cup (1 stick) butter in small bowl in a microwave or in a small saucepan over medium heat and let cool. Finely grate zest of whole lemon, then squeeze 2 Tbsp. juice; set aside.

Brush 1 Tbsp. melted butter around bottom and sides of cake pan (if you don’t have a pastry brush, use your fingertips). Line bottom of pan with a circle of parchment paper, smoothing to eliminate air bubbles between parchment and pan. Making a parchment circle is easy: just place your pan on a piece of parchment, trace the circumference with a pencil, then cut it out.
→  Don't have a cake pan? Drop everything and buy this one.

Toss cherries, 2 Tbsp. sugar, 2 Tbsp. lemon juice, and 2 Tbsp. melted butter in cake pan (fruit will clump up when it hits the melted butter, which is fine). It’ll all melt together as it bakes.
→  Wanna swap in frozen blueberries for those cherries? This brand is our favorite.
Bake cherries, gently shaking pan once halfway through, until fruit juices are thick and bubbling around sides of pan, 40–50 minutes. Let cool. Reduce oven temperature to 350°.
Whisk 2 cups flour, 2 tsp. baking powder, ½ tsp. salt, ¼ tsp. baking soda, and 1 cup sugar in a large bowl.

Make a well in the center of dry ingredients. Beat 3 eggs to blend and pour into center of well along with 1 cup sour cream, 2 tsp. lemon zest, and remaining 5 Tbsp. melted butter (it’s okay if butter has re-solidified; go ahead and scrape it in there).
Starting in the center, whisk to blend egg mixture, then make larger circles outward to incorporate dry ingredients. Whisk several more times until batter is smooth.
Carefully dollop large spoonfuls of batter over fruit (it’s okay if pan is still warm and some fruit juices pool around batter); gently smooth surface.
Bake cake until top is golden brown and a tester or toothpick inserted into the center comes out clean, 45–55 minutes.
Let cake cool in pan 10 minutes, then use a paring knife to loosen cake from edge of pan.

Place a wire rack over pan, then swiftly invert rack and pan; cake should release from pan on its own. Lift cake pan off cake, peel off parchment, and marvel at your creation. If some of the cherries cling to the parchment, tuck them back into place. Let cake cool completely.
Whisk remaining 1 cup sour cream with 1–2 Tbsp. sugar in a large bowl. Dollop on each slice before serving.

one-pan-lasagna.name: One-pan Lasagna
instructions: Preheat oven to 350° F (180°C).
Heat the oil in a cast-iron skillet over high heat.
Cook the beef, salt, and pepper until the moisture has evaporated and the beef is starting to brown.
Add the garlic and onion and cook until onions are starting to become translucent.
Add one cup (260 g) of the marinara sauce and cook until the sauce has reduced to a paste.

Using a large spoon or a spatula, push some of the beef mixture toward the side of the pan, creating a gap that allows you to slide lasagna sheets underneath the beef. Break about two lasagna sheets in order to fill in the cracks.
Top the beef mixture with the rest of the lasagna sheets. Spread the rest of the marinara sauce on top of the sheets. Dollop the ricotta on top of the sauce and spread evenly.
Sprinkle the mozzarella cheese over the ricotta, and top with the basil.
Bake for 25 minutes until cheese is bubbly and golden brown.
Cool, slice, then serve.
Enjoy!

one-pot-mediterranean-chicken.name: One-pot Mediterranean Chicken
instructions: Melt butter in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Add onion, garlic, Italian seasoning, basil, and salt; sauté until starting to brown, about 5 minutes.

Add artichoke hearts, crushed tomatoes, diced tomatoes, olives, and mushrooms; bring to a boil. Add chicken, cover skillet, and reduce heat to medium. Cook, stirring occasionally, until chicken is cooked through and the juices run clear, 20 to 25 minutes.

one-pot-pasta-primavera.name: One-pot Pasta Primavera
instructions: In a pot over high heat, combine broth, linguine, broccoli, asparagus, onion, garlic, red pepper flakes, salt, pepper, and olive oil. Stir.
Add the mushrooms and bring to a boil.
Once at a boil, reduce heat to medium and cook the pasta for 10 minutes, continuously tossing with tongs.
Add the heavy cream, parmesan, and parsley, and toss for an additional 3 minutes.
If the liquid hasn’t been completely absorbed, let the pasta sit off the heat for an additional few minutes.
Enjoy!

onion-steaks-teleculinaria.name: Onion Steaks
instructions: Tempere as bifanas com sal, pimenta, os alhos esmagados, o vinho branco, a folha de louro e o colorau e envolva tudo muito bem. Deixe marinar durante algumas horas.
Leve ao lume uma frigideira com o azeite. Junte as bifanas bem escorridas e deixe alourar de ambos os lados. Retire a carne da frigideira e reserve.
In the same pan, pour the butter, let it melt and then add the onions cut into thin half-moons. Water with the marinade and wrap it around, letting it cook until the onion is softened.
Sirva as bifanas com a cebolada por cima, polvilhando tudo com os pickles e a salsa picados. Acrescente as azeitonas e sirva com arroz branco cozido.

orange-kumquat-marmalade.name: Orange-kumquat Marmalade
instructions: Using a knife, remove peel from orange. Set orange aside. Cut white pith from peel. Place peel in a medium saucepan and add cold water to cover by 1”. Bring to a boil; drain. Repeat 2 more times. Let cool slightly.

Finely chop peel and reserved orange; place in a medium saucepan and add sugar, kumquats, red pepper flakes, black pepper, and 2 cups water. Bring to a boil, reduce heat, and simmer, stirring occasionally, until citrus is soft and water is evaporated, 35–45 minutes. Let cool; mix in orange juice.
DO AHEAD: Marmalade can be made 2 weeks ahead. Cover and chill.

orecchiette-with-cauliflower-anchovies-a.name: Orecchiette With Cauliflower, Anchovies, And Fried Croutons

ingredients.item: Orecchiette (little ear-shaped pasta) or medium-size shell pasta
(packed) freshly grated parmesan cheese plus additional for serving

instructions: Preheat oven to 218°C. Toss cauliflower florets with 1 tablespoon olive oil in large bowl to coat. Sprinkle with salt and pepper and spread in single layer on large rimmed baking sheet. Roast until cauliflower florets are tender and beginning to brown in spots, stirring occasionally, about 25 minutes. Cool to room temperature. DO AHEAD Can be made 1 day ahead. Cover and chill.

Cook pasta in large pot of boiling generously salted water until just tender but still firm to bite, stirring occasionally. Drain pasta, reserving 1 1/3 cups pasta cooking liquid. Set pasta and cooking liquid aside separately.

Meanwhile, heat 2 tablespoons olive oil in large deep nonstick skillet or large pot over medium-high heat. Add bread cubes and sauté until golden brown and crisp, 3 to 4 minutes. Transfer to small bowl and cool. Reserve skillet or pot.

Add 2 tablespoons olive oil to same skillet or pot and heat over medium-high heat. Add zucchini and garlic and sauté until zucchini is golden brown and crisp-tender, 3 to 4 minutes. Add cauliflower and anchovies and sauté until heated through, 3 to 4 minutes. Season to taste with salt and freshly ground black pepper. Add drained pasta, reserved 1 1/3 cups pasta cooking liquid, remaining 3 tablespoons olive oil, 1/3 cup parsley, 1/3 cup Parmesan, and Pecorino Romano cheese and toss to coat. Season pasta to taste with salt and freshly ground black pepper. Divide pasta among 6 bowls; sprinkle with fried croutons, remaining parsley, and additional Parmesan cheese and serve.

Fried zucchini blossoms make a nice garnish for the pasta. Toss the blossoms in flour to coat, then fry them in a large skillet filled with 1/2 cup olive oil heated to 182°C just until they're golden. Drain the fried blossoms on paper towels before placing them on top of the pasta.

oregano-chicken-argiro.name: Oregano Chicken
instructions: Add the meat to olive oil and garlic for 7'-8'.
Add the lemon juice.
Add the wine and 1 cup of water.
Once the chicken is boiled, lower the heat and simmer for 1 hour and 15 minutes until it softens.
In the meantime, add salt and pepper.
Towards the end of the boiling time sprinkle with oregano.
When we bring it down from the fire, the food should stay with very little sauce, almost with its oil.
Serve with the vegetable oatmeal.

oreo-cupcakes-with-white-chocolate-butte.name: Oreo Cupcakes With White Chocolate Buttercream
oreo-cupcakes-with-white-chocolate-butte.original_name: Cupcakes de Oreo con buttercream de chocolate blanco

ingredients.item: 115 g de mantequilla sin sal a temperatura ambiente
1 cucharadita y media de levadura royal
250 g de mantequilla sin sal a temperatura ambiente
250 g sugar icing (or, if applicable, glas sugar)

instructions: We purify the oven at 180º (160º if with a fan). We prepared the cupcake plate with 12 paper capsules (I have been using an oven porcelain plate, simply put them up before using them). We toss the flour with the chemical yeast into a bowl and reserve. We batilate butter with sugar until they are integrated and the mixture clear. We add the eggs, one by one, beating them up. We add half the flour and beat at low speed till it's incorporated. Then we add milk, mixed with the vanilla extract, and we bawl again. We add the other half of the flour and beat at low velocity until the mixture is homogenous. We put the pieces of Oreo cookies with a scatula.

We distribute the mixture into the capsules without more than 2 / 3. We bake 22-25 minutes or till a stick's clear. We let the cupcakes cool at a 5-minute mold and then we get onto a grid, till they get completely cold.
When cold, we put them out and filled them with chocolate syrup.

oysters-with-parmesan-polenta-crust.name: Oysters With Parmesan-polenta Crust

ingredients.item: Ground espelette pepper or other mild ground chile pepper

instructions: Place the eggs in a shallow bowl and whisk to blend. In a second shallow bowl, combine the polenta, cheese, salt, and pepper.

Pour the oil into the saucepan or deep-fryer to a depth of at least 2 inches. If using a deep-fry thermometer to take the temperature of the oil, attach it to the side of the saucepan. Heat the oil over medium heat to 191°C, or until a wooden chopstick inserted into the oil begins to fizz with bubbles. Do not be tempted to use too high a heat to bring the oil to temperature, as it will be harder to control and keep stable once you arrive at your goal temperature.

When the oil has come to temperature, coat the oysters: Dredge the oysters first in the eggs, allowing any excess to drip back into the bowl, then in the polenta mixture, shaking off any excess. Arrange on a platter. (The oysters should be fried within just a few moments of coating them.)

Fry the oysters in batches, about three at a time, until the breading is crisp, firm, and deep golden, 2 to 3 minutes. Remove with the wire skimmer and transfer to the paper towels to drain. Serve immediately with lemon wedges and aïoli or lime mayonnaise.
